title: carya texana var. villosa
intro: Carya texana var. villosa, also known as the Texas pecan or hairy pecan, is a variety of pecan tree native to North America, specifically the southwestern United States and Mexico. The bark and leaves of the Texas pecan have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Carya texana var. villosa are said to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of diabetes. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
